CIPHER.194.cipherString: Working together is of utmost importance as one country alone cannot address all the challenges.
CIPHER.194.question: This code is found written in chalk on the sidewalk outside the science building. It's a quote by Atanasio Amisse Canira, written in an Aristocrat Cipher. What does it say?
CIPHER.194.author: Atanasio Amisse Canira

CIPHER.173.cipherString: Great interests are apt to clash with each other.
CIPHER.173.question: Upon searching a room, the following were found on scraps of paper. You are told it's a quote by Lucan encoded as a Nihilist Substitution with a keyword of WORN and polybius key of EXHAUSTING. What does it say?
CIPHER.173.author: Lucan

CIPHER.174.cipherString: You've achieved success in your field when you don't know whether what you're doing is work or play.
CIPHER.174.question: A weathered note is found inside an old science textbook. The writing appears to be a quote by Warren Beatty encoded as an Aristocrat using a K1 alphabet. You are told that the cipher contains the sequence YOU four times. What does it say?
CIPHER.174.author: Warren Beatty

CIPHER.170.cipherString: It's not really a rule--its more like a trend.
CIPHER.170.question: Your lava lamp started bubbling in Baconian code by Larry Wall. You are told that the deciphered text starts with ITSN. What does it say?
CIPHER.170.author: Larry Wall

CIPHER.190.cipherString: Everything makes sense a bit at a time. But when you try to think of it all at once, it comes out wrong.
CIPHER.190.question: Written in glow-in-the-dark ink on the ceiling of the bio lab, uncover this Aristocrat quote by Terry Pratchett. What does it say?
CIPHER.190.author: Terry Pratchett

CIPHER.177.cipherString: We don't like their sound, and guitar music is on the way out.
CIPHER.177.question: Etched into the surface of a melted vinyl record, you found this Complete Columnar-encrypted quote by Decca Recording Company. The quote has MUSIC somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.177.author: Decca Recording Company

CIPHER.182.cipherString: The preservation of liberty depends upon the intellectual and moral character of the people.
CIPHER.182.question: This coded note is discovered inside a sealed envelope labeled “Confidential.” It's a quote by David McCullough, encoded using an Aristocrat Cipher. What does it say?
CIPHER.182.author: David McCullough

CIPHER.185.cipherString: Truth uttered before its time is always dangerous.
CIPHER.185.question: You came across the following written on a wall in a cave. You are told that it's a quote by Mencius encoded as a Nihilist Substitution with a keyword of MAINLY and Polybius key of MISFORTUNE. What does it say?
CIPHER.185.author: Mencius

CIPHER.188.cipherString: Quality in a classical Greek sense is how to live with grace and intelligence, with bravery and mercy.
CIPHER.188.question: A time-capsule USB drive from 1983 just auto-opened with this Aristocrat-encrypted message by Theodore White using a K1 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.188.author: Theodore White

CIPHER.181.cipherString: For me the time passes slowly and joyously away.
CIPHER.181.question: A laser-etched message on the inside of a geodes crystal reads: solve this Baconian version of Horace's wisdom. What does it say?
CIPHER.181.author: Horace

CIPHER.179.cipherString: He that speaks the truth will find himself in sufficiently dramatic situations.
CIPHER.179.question: During a field trip, you notice these strange markings on a metal plaque. They represent a quote by Prof. Wilson, encoded using a Complete Columnar Cipher. The quote has TRUTH somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.179.author: Prof. Wilson

CIPHER.189.cipherString: Birth, ancestry, and what we have ourselves not done, I would hardly call our own.
CIPHER.189.question: A strange code appears in the footer of a document you print from the lab computer. It's a quote by Ovid, encoded in a Checkerboard with a polybius key of MEASURING. What does it say?
CIPHER.189.author: Ovid

CIPHER.192.cipherString: I'll never second guess the things that I have done. I've got too much left to say and too much to become.
CIPHER.192.question: Someone left this coded message in the school lab. You are told it's a quote by Alex Gaskarth encoded with an Aristocrat using a K1 alphabet. What was the K1 Keyword used to encode it?
CIPHER.192.author: Alex Gaskarth

CIPHER.175.cipherString: Memories are made in the creation of fictional characters.
CIPHER.175.question: Your drone photographed some weird markings in a parking lot. Turns out it's a quote by Mitta Xinindlu in a Baconian. What does it say?
CIPHER.175.author: Mitta Xinindlu

CIPHER.186.cipherString: The scientific theory I like best is that the rings of Saturn are composed entirely of lost airline luggage.
CIPHER.186.question: The message below was intercepted from an experimental satellite transmission. It contains a quote by Mark Russell, encoded as an Aristocrat using a K2 alphabet. You are told that the cipher contains THAT. What was the K2 Keyword used to encode it?
CIPHER.186.author: Mark Russell

CIPHER.168.question: A coded message is discovered inside a fortune cookie. It turns out to be a Motto, encoded using a Hill Cipher with a keyword of TURN. What does it say?
CIPHER.168.author: Motto

CIPHER.184.cipherString: Competition is great. And as long as it's friendly and not a malicious thing, then I think it's cool.
CIPHER.184.question: A riddle is discovered taped under a chemistry lab bench. It's a quote by Janet Jackson, encrypted as an Aristocrat. You are told that the cipher contains the word AS twice.  What does it say?
CIPHER.184.author: Janet Jackson

CIPHER.183.question: You uncover this encoded note tucked behind a microscope slide. It's a Cryptarithm. What do the values 569371 0134 7281 decode to?

CIPHER.178.cipherString: Soy muy educado por naturaleza, incluso las voces en mi cabeza se dejan terminar sus frases.
CIPHER.178.question: Scrawled in dry-erase marker on the whiteboard after hours: this Xenocrypt encoded quote by Graham Parke in Spanish using a K1 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.178.author: Graham Parke
CIPHER.178.translation: I'm very polite by nature, even the voices in my head let each other finish their sentences.

CIPHER.171.question: While repairing an old telescope, you find a note hidden in the tube. The message is a quote written in a Cryptarithm. What do the values 6410 8173 46259 decode to?

CIPHER.187.cipherString: Search others for their virtues, and thyself for thy vices.
CIPHER.187.question: Your school-issued Chromebook's login screen hijacked itself with this Complete Columnar quote from Fuller. The quote has THEIR somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.187.author: Fuller

CIPHER.169.cipherString: Brass shines with constant usage, a beautiful dress needs wearing; Leave a house empty, it rots.
CIPHER.169.question: You found the following carved into the bark of a hollow log. You are told that it's a quote by Ovid encoded as an Aristocrat. You are told that the cipher ends with rots. What does it say?
CIPHER.169.author: Ovid

CIPHER.191.cipherString: Our attitude towards others determines their attitude towards us.
CIPHER.191.question: Someone left this encrypted quote scribbled on the chalkboard before class began. It's a quote by Earl Nightingale, written as a Porta using a keyword of SECOND. What does it say?
CIPHER.191.author: Earl Nightingale

CIPHER.176.cipherString: The artist thought his favourite paint had been stolen, but it was just a pigment of his imagination.
CIPHER.176.question: A mysterious balloon release at the science fair carried this Aristocrat banner using a K2 alphabet. You are told that the cipher contains the word his twice. What does it say?
CIPHER.176.author: Unknown

CIPHER.193.cipherString: Self-denial is indulgence of a propensity to forgo.
CIPHER.193.question: Your science fair project logs contain a mysterious string of letters. It's a quote by Ambrose Bierce, encoded as a Fractionated Morse. You are told that the quote starts with SELFDE. What does it say?
CIPHER.193.author: Ambrose Bierce

CIPHER.172.cipherString: Anonymity gives people power to do wrong things, without facing the consequences of their actions.
CIPHER.172.question: Hidden in the margin of a library book on black holes: decode this Aristocrat version of De philosopher DJ Kyos using a K2 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.172.author: De philosopher DJ Kyos

CIPHER.180.cipherString: The day you start telling yourself you can, is the day you start knowing you will.
CIPHER.180.question: A note falls out of a borrowed library book. The message is a quote by Steve Maraboli, written as a Checkerboard Cipher encoded with a Polybius key of EXHAUSTING. What does it say?
CIPHER.180.author: Steve Maraboli
